summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #289: the framework-agnostic DatePicker state machine (agnostic core only, no adapters) pairing a text input with an embedded Calendar in a popover, including full ARIA, data attrs, value/open controlled-prop sync, and TDD test coverage.
summary.outcome: PR #697 opened on fogodev/ars-ui with 5 commits: the DatePicker core, ars-i18n date_order exposure, xtask snapshot-gate fixes, Codex review remediation (passes 1–4 fully resolved, pass 5 partially resolved). The PR is green on all 22 CI checks but work is still in progress: pass-5 finding P5-4 (focus-intent on controlled-open SyncProps) was deferred pending a holistic design review, and the pass-5 clear fixes (P5-1/2/3) were being committed at the end of the transcript.

repo: ars-i18n already had a private `date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` with CLDR (icu4x), JS Intl (web-intl/wasm), and (language,region) fallback paths — both DateField and DatePicker had been reimplementing only the fallback heuristic; the fix was to make it public.

CalendarDate has no PartialOrd/Ord — comparisons must use `.compare(m) -> Ordering`; fields are `pub(crate)` so accessors `year()/month()/day()` are required; `new_gregorian(i32,u8,u8)` returns `Result`, not a direct value; `nzu8` helper does not exist anywhere.

The Machine trait requires `type Effect: EffectName` — string effect names and `use_platform_effects()` closures in core don't compile. Named focus effects (`PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`) are the correct pattern; adapters execute actual DOM focus.
Service::new takes props and env by reference (`&`), and `send` is `#[must_use]` — must `drop(svc.send(...))` in tests.
ComponentIds has no public `base_id` field; the accessor is `ids.id()`.

Context derives `#[derive(Clone)]` + manual `impl Debug` (not PartialEq) — Messages holds MessageFn (Arc pointer equality PartialEq) which makes Context PartialEq unsound. Calendar establishes this convention.

The snapshot-count xtask gate silently skipped the entire `date_time` and `data_display` categories because `is_component_category` matched hyphenated dir names ('date-time') while the actual source dirs and snapshot paths use underscores ('date_time').

`is_ars_components_component` (which sets the 3-per-variant floor) had the same hyphen/underscore bug — this caused rating_group to incorrectly require 9 snapshots (9 = 3 variants × 3/variant) instead of 3 (1 variant × 3/variant).

Controlled-open semantics: Popover commits state locally on user events and relies on `on_open_change` + parent re-prop — but after Codex review, the DatePicker was changed to block state mutation when open is controlled (pure React-controlled model), requiring a `requested_open` context field.

For controlled value notifications, the repo convention (rating_group) is a `requested_value` context field + `Effect::ValueChange` named marker — NOT a Props callback — so the adapter reads the requested value and notifies upstream.
`Callback::new_ref` (not `Callback::new`) is required for higher-ranked `for<'a> Fn(&'a T)` form, e.g., `IsDateUnavailableFn`.

The CI Snapshot Change Check only fires on `pull_request: [opened, synchronize, reopened]` — it does NOT re-fire on `labeled`. Adding `snapshot-reviewed` label after PR creation leaves the check red; a manual re-run is needed.
`spec-compile-snippets` does `syn` syntax-only parsing — type/borrow errors are out of scope; blocks can opt out with `rust,no_check`.

code.finding: Private `date_order(locale)` function with cfg-selected CLDR (icu_date_order via ICU4X probe date), JS Intl (js_date_order via Intl.DateTimeFormat.formatToParts), and (language,region) heuristic fallback. Exposed as pub in this session.
to_iso8601() returns canonical YYYY-MM-DD string — the correct format for hidden input values (not display-calendar year()/month()/day() fields).

is_ars_components_component listed hyphenated dir names ('date-time', 'data-display') but filesystem dirs are underscored — caused wrong floor calculations for all date_time and data_display components. Fixed in this session.
Canonical sync_props_into_ctx helper + Bindable::sync_controlled pattern for controlled/uncontrolled prop re-derivation on SyncProps events. DatePicker mirrors this exactly.
on_trigger_keydown returns bool (handled status) to prevent double-toggle from keydown + native click. DatePicker's on_trigger_keydown was updated to match.
Establishes the ValueChange convention: named Effect::ValueChange marker + requested_value context field (no Props callback). Adapter reads requested_value and notifies upstream.

workflow: TDD discipline: write failing tests first (logic tests excluding snapshots), then implement, then generate+review snapshots with `cargo insta test` / `cargo insta accept` / `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

post-implementation-audit skill runs 3 phases: spec/impl drift scan, iterative completeness critic (rounds until dry), and llvm-cov coverage with gap analysis — all findings must be landed in the same PR.
The waiting-for-codex-review skill loop: post `@codex review`, poll for terminal verdict (👍 or 👀-dropped), triage findings with TDD, push fix, reply inline + resolve threads, re-post `@codex review`.
cargo xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate (10 gates); full cargo xci adds wasm-coverage and feature-matrix groups and is needed before merging PRs that touch i18n/cross-backend code.
When a Codex review re-litigates a prior decision (like controlled-open semantics), check with the user before implementing — the decision may be reversed, generating cascading changes.
Snapshot-count gate and spec-compile-snippets are xtask gates run as part of xci-fast — broken gates in those tools silently give false passes until the tool itself is fixed.
A proptest with controlled/uncontrolled prop toggling is a high-value invariant test — it caught a real Bindable internal-state divergence bug that unit tests missed.

friction: Spec §1.7 had ~10 compile-blockers vs the actual codebase (string effects, missing `type Effect`, nonexistent `nzu8`, CalendarDate field access, non-Ord comparisons, wrong `is_date_unavailable` type, missing `on_props_changed`/`initial_effects`, by-value Service::new) — required extensive API archaeology before any code could be written.

A property test caught a real bug (open Bindable diverging from State on controlled→uncontrolled flip) that required removing the `open` Bindable entirely and making State the single source of truth — a significant mid-implementation refactor.

The snapshot-count xtask gate silently skipped two entire categories (date_time, data_display) due to a hyphen/underscore mismatch — the gate gave false passes on the entire date-time subsystem until fixed.
A stale staged change in pagination/mod.rs (from before the session) broke xci-fast until identified and fixed as a drive-by.
CI Snapshot Change Check ran before the snapshot-reviewed label was added (structural ordering race) — required a manual job re-run to clear the red check.
Codex review ran 5 passes with 8+5+4+3+4 findings; pass 4 reversed the controlled-open model chosen in pass 1, generating follow-on focus-flow complexity (P5-4) that required a design pause.
Closure lifetime errors in tests when using temporary closures in assertions — required binding to `let` before use.
Session limit was hit mid-work twice, requiring 'continue' prompts to resume.
open_items: P5-4 deferred: focus-intent tracking for controlled-open SyncProps — when open is controlled, SyncProps currently always emits FocusCalendar on open / RestoreFocusToTrigger on close, losing the originating intent (a FocusIn-triggered open should keep focus in the input; a SelectDate close should restore to input). Needs new machinery (carried focus-intent field) after holistic design review.

Holistic controlled-state design review needed: the controlled-open and controlled-value models went through multiple reversals across Codex passes; a single coherent design doc should be reviewed before final merge.
Full `cargo xci` (not just xci-fast) should be run before merging since the PR includes i18n foundation changes (date_order exposure) that add the wasm-coverage and feature-matrix groups.
Pre-existing utility/highlight snapshot orphans still block a bare `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` — unrelated to this PR but a latent CI hazard.

CI timing race: Snapshot Change Check doesn't re-trigger on `labeled` events — needs either adding `labeled`/`unlabeled` to pull_request trigger types (expensive: re-runs all CI) or switching snapshot acknowledgment to CHANGELOG entries (first-push compatible).

IntlBackend trait still doesn't expose date field-order as a method — `date_order()` is now public in ars-i18n but components call it directly rather than through the trait abstraction. A proper trait method + both backend impls would be a cleaner foundation but was scoped as a separate task.

Adapter specs (Leptos/Dioxus) for DatePicker exist and reference calendar_props() correctly but haven't been updated to reflect the new controlled-open semantics (requested_open, block-when-controlled model) — should be reviewed before adapter implementation begins.
